channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gamedeveloper.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gamedeveloper.com

item.title: Report: Xbox adds Matthew Ball and Scott Van Vliet to leadership team
item.content:encoded:
Game industry analyst Matthew Ball and Scott Van Vliet are the latest hires to the Xbox leadership team (thanks, The Game Business). Ball, well-known for the State of Video Gaming Report, will take on the mantle of chief strategy officer. He's tasked with boosting the console side of Xbox in the current stormy environment.

Scott Van Vliet, formerly the lead of Azure OpenAI and AI Core infrastructure at Microsoft, will take on the chief technology officer role. He has a background that includes posts at Amazon (including at Amazon Game Studios) and in the Digital Play arena at Mattel.

As noted in the report, Chris Schnakenberg has also been promoted to corporate vice president of partnerships & business development.

The moves all come within months of Xbox CEO Asha Sharma taking over from longtime CEO Phil Spencer. Earlier this month, Sharma confirmed other new appointments (including former CoreAI leaders Jared Palmer, Evan Chaka, and Tim Allen), the nixing of Copilot AI on the console, and a directive to "move faster."

Related:Ubisoft's net bookings are down 54% year-on-year

About the Author

Editor-in-Chief, GameDeveloper.com

Danielle is the editor-in-chief of Game Developer, with previous editorial posts at Fanbyte, VICE, and Polygon. She’s also a lecturer in game design at the Berklee College of Music, and a hobbyist game developer in her spare time.



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/report-xbox-adds-matthew-ball-and-scott-van-vliet-to-leadership-team
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Hasbro cancels Dungeons & Dragons game led by Respawn veterans
item.content:encoded:
Image via Dungeons & Dragons and Giant Skull

The collaboration between Wizards of the Coast (WOTC), a Hasbro subsidiary, and developer Giant Skull, a studio founded by Respawn veteran Stig Asmussen, has come to a close less than a year after the announcement.

This has been confirmed by WOTC itself, which shared the news in a statement to Bloomberg. The companies were working on an action-adventure game set in the Dungeons & Dragons universe, which, at the time of its reveal, was deemed a "definitive moment in both companies' gaming ambitions."

As it turns out, WOTC pulled out of the deal earlier this year. "We assess concepts at every stage of development," a spokesperson told Bloomberg.

Moreover, the company added that, while it decided not to pursue an early concept from Giant Skull, it retains "great respect for Stig Asmussen and his team and value our ongoing relationship."

As for Asmussen himself, he told Bloomberg that he had enjoyed working with WOTC, and that he was talking to people there and at other companies about potential new publishing deals.

Related:Embracer chairman says cost control 'does not get enough respect' after presiding over years of layoffs

"Things are good at Giant Skull," he told the publication.

"Stig and the team at Giant Skull are exactly the type of exceptionally talented creators we want to work with, and I'm so happy to be reuniting with him on this new project," president of WOTC and digital gaming at Hasbro John Hight said back in June of last year.

Asmussen's team was established in 2024, formed by several Respawn Entertainment alumni and a number of former Fortnite developers.

When asked about his decision to leave a relatively stable operation at Respawn to pursue a new studio venture, Asmussen said the following: "One of my main mottos is 'no regrets. I didn't want to be in a situation where I decided to stay put and a year or two years from now, I'd be looking over my shoulder and be like 'what about that other thing I could have done?'"

Giant Skull's game isn't the only project that Hasbro has axed this year. Back in February, a company spokesperson confirmed the closure of Atomic Arcade, the studio established by WOTC and Hasbro. Yet, it also said the studio's unnamed Snake Eyes project has not been canceled.

About the Author

Contributing Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

Diego Nicolás Argüello is a freelance journalist and critic from Argentina. Video games helped him to learn English, so now he covers them for places like The New York Times, NPR, Rolling Stone, and more. He also runs Into the Spine, a site dedicated to fostering and supporting new writers, and co-hosted Turnabout Breakdown, a podcast about the Ace Attorney series. He’s most likely playing a rhythm game as you read this.



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/hasbro-cancels-dungeons-dragons-game-led-by-respawn-veteran
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
